title: Religions in NGE
wikitext:
[]: Religions in NGE             Quote Post by Tominator2 » Tue Oct 25, 2005 9:47 pm Along the lines of [url]http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=780[/url], what religion(s) would it make sense for a character in the NGE universe to be? To be a little more specific (and throw epistemology to the wind) let's say you live in the NGE universe and go to Fuyutski's big demo where he sucks a soul out of an audience member and transfers it into a Real Doll . He can reverse this, and do it repeatedly. You're convinced. What happens to your religious beliefs? The asoulists :oops: (some ancient Greeks, some strains of Judaism) are in trouble. Atheists would seem to be on the ropes. Some Buddhists might be in trouble. What about the other major religions?

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Tue Oct 25, 2005 11:09 pm That's kind of hard to say what the characters in Evangelion would be, religious wise. Perhaps Buddhists or Taoists. I'm guessing that because I THINK that those are the two most practiced religions in Japan. Then again, even that's hard to say, due to what happened in the past... And as for myself if I saw that happen... I wouldn't change my beliefs at all. I have no religion, simple enough. I have my own personal beliefs and whether they are similar to those that people believe in a religion doesn't matter to me or not. I believe in spirits, ghosts, other plains of reality and dimensions. So, if I am to believe in ghosts, I need to believe in souls. Seeing a soul get taking out of a human and placed into a doll, seeing the doll come to life, would be shocking, but it wouldn't make me change my beliefs... Only secure them.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Wed Oct 26, 2005 12:13 am I imagine that the characters in NGE are agnostic by default, as they are really trying to find out the nature of God and humanity through science. The metaphysical experience that they have points to something like Gnostic Christianity, if you want to get technical, but it isn't quite the same. That being said, their thinking, moral and otherwise, is clearly shaped by the frame of the Eastern religions: Taoism, Buddhism, Confucianism, Shintoism, and so on.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 28, 2005 5:43 am When what was formerly metaphysics or theology becomes engineering, it is no longer a matter of faith. Pure non-dualist materialism would fall given the new physics - but until there was an experimental result for or against any deity, atheism would still be tenable.

[]:             Quote Post by K2Grey » Sat Oct 29, 2005 1:02 am I too don't see why the atheists would be on the rope, unless Fuyutsuki could prove the existance of a deity while he was at it. But since this soul transfer technique is technological, it's kinda irrelevant. Now, atheism might have some trouble with Adam and Lilith, but it shouldn't be too big seeing as how these Sources of Life can be tampered with in ways usually inconsistent with deities.

[]:             Quote Post by Tominator2 » Mon Oct 31, 2005 11:24 pm Pure non-dualist materialism would fall given the new physics

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Tue Nov 01, 2005 1:52 am Tominator2 wrote: Pure non-dualist materialism would fall given the new physics

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Nov 01, 2005 1:30 pm Tominator2 wrote: Pure non-dualist materialism would fall given the new physics

[]:             Quote Post by insight » Thu Nov 10, 2005 7:12 pm It's hard to say what the Eva Crew, if you will, believe. They see some pretty strange stuff that doesn't seem to phase them very much. They are just "normal" people making breakthroughs in science. Since no one mentions it, or really shows their belief, it is hard to find a "religion". They are people in a fantasy world who live with things bizarre to us (giant humanoids, attacking giant "things") and they act as if it is normal. EDIT: Religion IMHO is an overrated term. When the average person thinks or religion, they think of someone devoting their life to a cause that will somehow improve their afterlife, or something to that affect. A person can be religious and not even show it, just believe in what they think will happen, since nobody really knows for sure. Or maybe they're religious but they don't even know what their belief is. The folks in NGE may be religious, but not even bother to talk about it, I mean, why should they talk about where they think they'll go when NERV is under high stress while being attacked by an Angel. run-on
